evidences.title: CTBC Flying Oysters Shock T1 with a Stunning Upset at Worlds ...
evidences.content: In the end, T1 tried to fight back, but unfortunately for them and their fans, CFO played flawlessly. A clean victory for CFO, putting them at 2-0 in the Swiss Stage, while T1 drop to a 1-1 record. Reminder: the Swiss Stage at Worlds 2025 runs from October 15–19 and 22–25. Sixteen teams play five rounds in a Swiss format: teams with 3 wins advance to the Knockout Stage, and teams with 3 losses are eliminated. Elimination and advancement matches are Best-of-Three with Fearless Draft. All other matches are Best-of-One, so every game matters. [...] # CTBC Flying Oysters claim an incredible victory over T1 at the Worlds 2025 Swiss Stage The next matchup was truly worth every second of attention — T1 vs CTBC Flying Oysters. At first glance, it looked like an easy and predictable win for T1, but what CFO managed to pull off was simply unbelievable. Let’s take a quick look at how the game went. Game 1 Picks: CFO controlled the game from start to finish. Their fast and precise play allowed them to consistently find picks on T1 and steadily build their lead. One of the key moments was a lane swap — Draven moved to the top lane, where he managed to secure a solo kill on Rek’Sai. Doggo ’s...

evidences.title: CTBC Flying Oyster - Wikipedia
evidences.content: CFO finished the mid-season regular season undefeated with seven series wins. The team narrowly defeated Vietnam's GAM Esports 3–2 to win their second LCP title and a spot in the main event of the 2025 Mid-Season Invitational (MSI 2025), the first time they qualified for the tournament. In the main event bracket stage of MSI 2025, CFO was knocked out in the second round of the lower bracket by Chinese team Anyone's Legend. evidences.title: Team CTBC Flying Oyster LoL - EGamersWorld
evidences.content: ## The results of CTBC Flying Oyster in PCS Having appeared in 2022 and received a slot in PCS, CTBC Flying Oyster had a debut season the same year. The first roster of Flying Oyster included Rest, Gemini, Mission, Atlen, and Koala. Archie was the main coach, he used to work for Bilibili Gaming, J Team, and Taipei Assassins, and Kevil Miller was his assistant. ### PCS Spring 2022 PCS Spring 2022 was the debut championship for CTBC Flying Oyster. It became clear that the tea had a big potential. It started appearing with the start of the regular season of the Taiwanese league. During the Single-Round Robin regular league, CTBC Flying Oyster showed a decent level of play, having finished the group stage in 3rd place. [...] The top 3 allowed CTBC Flying Oyster to advance to the upper playoff bracket of PCS Spring 2022. In the first two rounds, Flying Oyster defeated Meta Falcon Team with a score of 3-0 and then J Team with the same result. In the final of the upper bracket, CTBC Flying Oyster faced the thunderstorm of the region and the main obstacle for the team on the way to dominance within the region – Talon Esports. The little final of PCS Spring 2022 was over with a confident...

evidences.title: 2025 Six Metre World Championships
evidences.content: # 2025 Six Metre World Championships ## Home Welcome to the website for the International Six Metre World Championships 2025, hosted by the Seawanhaka Corinthian Yacht Club (SCYC), which is located on beautiful Centre Island in Oyster Bay, New York. The regatta runs from 19 to 26 September with Championship racing from 22 to 26 September. Further information about this event can be found on this website, and you can follow the event on our social media pages at Facebook and Instagram and via our news stories at 6metre.com A full entry list for the regatta, including links to find further information about each of the boats competing can be found here. Click here for the Official Notice Board where you will find the Notice of Race, Official Documents and in due course the Results.
